title: Gilia hugelia
intro: Gilia hugelia, also known as Oregon gilia or bear's ear, is a perennial plant in the borage family native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Gilia hugelia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
taxonomy: Gilia hugelia is classified within the family Polemoniaceae. This family belongs to the order Ericales, which is a part of the larger clade Asterids. The classification continues with the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants with two cotyledons. At the kingdom level, Gilia hugelia falls under Plantae, encompassing all plant species. Overall, the complete classification is Plantae > Magnoliopsida > Ericales > Polemoniaceae > Gilia > Gilia hugelia.

parts: Gilia hugelia, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily the leaves and flowers.
